chr21 <- keepSeqlevels(g, "chr21", pruning.mode = "coarse")
chr21
## GRanges object with 296 ranges and 1 metadata column:
##             seqnames            ranges strand |          GENEID
##                <Rle>         <IRanges>  <Rle> | <CharacterList>
##   100129027    chr21 47247755-47256333      - |       100129027
##   100131902    chr21 31661463-31661832      - |       100131902
##   100132288    chr21   9907189-9968593      - |       100132288
##   100133286    chr21 37441940-37498938      - |       100133286
##   100151643    chr21 31992946-31993169      + |       100151643
##         ...      ...               ...    ... .             ...
##        9619    chr21 43619799-43717354      + |            9619
##        9875    chr21 33683330-33765312      - |            9875
##        9946    chr21 34961648-35014160      - |            9946
##        9980    chr21 37536839-37666572      + |            9980
##        9992    chr21 35736323-35743440      + |            9992
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 1 sequence from hg19 genome
length(chr21)
## [1] 296
max(width(chr21))
## [1] 1196950
sum(strand(chr21) == "+")/length(chr21)
## [1] 0.4628378
chr21seq = getSeq(Hsapiens, chr21)
GC.cont = rowSums(letterFrequency(chr21seq, "GC"))/width(chr21seq)
median(GC.cont)
## [1] 0.4613219
chr21seq[5]
## DNAStringSet object of length 1:
##     width seq                                               names               
## [1]   224 TCCACAACTGAAACATCCACTTC...AGTTCAGTCTACTGCACTCCTCC 100151643
countPattern("ATG", chr21seq[[5]])
## [1] 6
start(matchPattern("ATG", chr21seq[[5]]))
## [1]  32  92 120 135 166 174
translate(chr21seq[[5]][32:width(chr21seq[5])])
## Warning in .Call2("DNAStringSet_translate", x, skip_code,
## dna_codes[codon_alphabet], : last base was ignored
## 64-letter AAString object
## seq: MSYYSHLSGGLGCGLAVAVTMGRTVAVAEYGRCRHGCHSSYSAR**HHGFH*GTPRAVQSTALL
snca <- genes(Homo.sapiens, filter=list(GENEID="6622"))
snca
## GRanges object with 1 range and 1 metadata column:
##        seqnames            ranges strand |          GENEID
##           <Rle>         <IRanges>  <Rle> | <CharacterList>
##   6622     chr4 90645250-90759447      - |            6622
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 93 sequences (1 circular) from hg19 genome
tss = resize(snca, 1)
tss
## GRanges object with 1 range and 1 metadata column:
##        seqnames    ranges strand |          GENEID
##           <Rle> <IRanges>  <Rle> | <CharacterList>
##   6622     chr4  90759447      - |            6622
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 93 sequences (1 circular) from hg19 genome
snca_seq = getSeq(Hsapiens, snca)
countPattern("ACTGTGAA",snca_seq[[1]]) + countPattern("ACTGTGAA",reverseComplement(snca_seq[[1]]))
## [1] 8
# OR
motif = "ACTGTGAA"
vcountPattern(motif, snca_seq) + vcountPattern(reverseComplement(DNAString(motif)), snca_seq)
## [1] 8
# Load the GRanges corresponding to human genes from the Homo.sapiens package
g <- genes(Homo.sapiens)
##   403 genes were dropped because they have exons located on both strands
##   of the same reference sequence or on more than one reference sequence,
##   so cannot be represented by a single genomic range.
##   Use 'single.strand.genes.only=FALSE' to get all the genes in a
##   GRangesList object, or use suppressMessages() to suppress this message.
# Load the ESRRA binding site GRanges in GM12878 cells
data(GM12878)

g[100]
## GRanges object with 1 range and 1 metadata column:
##             seqnames            ranges strand |          GENEID
##                <Rle>         <IRanges>  <Rle> | <CharacterList>
##   100113402    chr16 70563402-70563502      + |       100113402
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 93 sequences (1 circular) from hg19 genome
p = flank(g[100], 2000)
p.all=flank(g, 2000)

overlap = findOverlaps(p.all,GM12878)
length(unique(subjectHits(overlap)))
## [1] 757
length(unique(queryHits(overlap)))
## [1] 943
shift.p.all = shift(p.all,10000)
shift.overlap = findOverlaps(shift.p.all,GM12878)
length(unique(subjectHits(shift.overlap)))
## [1] 117
length(unique(subjectHits(overlap)))/length(unique(subjectHits(shift.overlap)))
## [1] 6.470085
